mcqs.question: What is the main difference between model-based and model-free methods?

options.text: Model-based methods require a predefined model of the environment, while model-free methods learn from trial and error by interacting with the environment.
Model-based methods learn from trial and error, while model-free methods require a predefined model.
Model-based methods are more flexible, while model-free methods are more scalable.
Model-based methods are more robust, while model-free methods are more sample efficient.

mcqs.explanation: Model-based methods require a predefined model of the environment, while model-free methods learn from trial and error by interacting with the environment.

mcqs.question: What is the Q-learning update rule?

options.text: Q(s, a) ← Q(s, a) + α[r + γmax(Q(s', a')) - Q(s, a)]
Q(s, a) ← Q(s, a) + α[r + γQ(s', a') - Q(s, a)]
Q(s, a) ← Q(s, a) + α[r - Q(s, a)]
Q(s, a) ← Q(s, a) + α[r + γmax(Q(s', a'))]

mcqs.explanation: The Q-learning update rule is Q(s, a) ← Q(s, a) + α[r + γmax(Q(s', a')) - Q(s, a)].

mcqs.question: What is the SARSA update rule?

options.text: Q(s, a) ← Q(s, a) + α[r + γQ(s', a') - Q(s, a)]
Q(s, a) ← Q(s, a) + α[r + γmax(Q(s', a')) - Q(s, a)]
Q(s, a) ← Q(s, a) + α[r - Q(s, a)]
Q(s, a) ← Q(s, a) + α[r + γmax(Q(s', a'))]

mcqs.explanation: The SARSA update rule is Q(s, a) ← Q(s, a) + α[r + γQ(s', a') - Q(s, a)].

mcqs.question: What are the advantages of model-free methods?

options.text: Flexibility, scalability, and robustness.
Sample efficiency, exploration-exploitation trade-off, and overfitting.
Flexibility, sample efficiency, and overfitting.
Scalability, exploration-exploitation trade-off, and robustness.

mcqs.explanation: The advantages of model-free methods are flexibility, scalability, and robustness.

options.text: Sample efficiency, exploration-exploitation trade-off, and overfitting.

Scalability, exploration-exploitation trade-off, and robustness.

mcqs.question: What is Deep Q-Networks (DQN)?

options.text: DQN is a type of Q-learning that uses a deep neural network to approximate the Q-function.
DQN is a type of SARSA that uses a deep neural network to approximate the Q-function.
DQN is a type of model-based method that uses a deep neural network to approximate the Q-function.
DQN is a type of model-free method that uses a shallow neural network to approximate the Q-function.

mcqs.explanation: DQN is a type of Q-learning that uses a deep neural network to approximate the Q-function.

mcqs.question: What is the exploration-exploitation trade-off?

options.text: The trade-off between exploring new actions and exploiting the current knowledge to maximize rewards.
The trade-off between exploiting the current knowledge and exploring new actions to maximize rewards.
The trade-off between exploring new actions and exploiting the current knowledge to minimize rewards.
The trade-off between exploiting the current knowledge and exploring new actions to minimize rewards.

mcqs.explanation: The exploration-exploitation trade-off is the trade-off between exploring new actions and exploiting the current knowledge to maximize rewards.

options.text: Robotics, game playing, and recommendation systems.
Robotics, game playing, and computer vision.

Robotics, computer vision, and natural language processing.
